$description: Web-terminal-only extension tokens: CRT-effect gates, the ASCII welcome modal's own muted-sage palette, and (added by Task 1.7 when the real build surfaced them as a gap) four theme-independent layout/motion constants plus one color tint that variables.css/theme-light.css define but Task 1.1 never tokenized — all four are live dependencies of scaffold.css/operator.css/terminal.css/files.css/settings.css/drawer.css/sessions.css/md-rendered.css/session.html (60+ call sites), so they cannot simply be dropped when those files are deleted. Namespaced --wt-* like every other extension here, deliberately NOT left as bare names (--header-height etc.) despite that costing a rename at every call site — bare names would reopen exactly the flat-namespace collision risk the wt-/art-/ariel-/lat-/cf- convention exists to close, and namespace-collision is a validated invariant (validate.py's check_namespace_collisions). Structured as top-level dark/light groups (one hop into core.json primitives, mirroring tokens/themes/*.json) — NOT semantic tokens, so they're namespaced --wt-* on emission. Source: web_terminal/static/css/variables.css + theme-light.css.

text-glow.$description: Composite text-shadow embedding green.200's rgb triplet at 25% alpha; kept literal (no one-hop alias expresses 'primitive + alpha').

wt-header-height.$description: Renamed from --header-height (Task 1.1 omission surfaced by the real build; used by terminal.css).

wt-transition-fast.$description: Renamed from --transition-fast; full transition shorthand (duration + easing), not a bare duration, so kept as a literal string rather than aliasing core.json's duration.instant primitive. Used 50+ times across drawer.css/terminal.css/files.css/settings.css/operator.css/md-rendered.css/scaffold.css/sessions.css/session.html.

wt-transition-normal.$description: Renamed from --transition-normal; same shorthand rationale as wt-transition-fast. Used by terminal.css, files.css, scaffold.css, session.html.

wt-accent-system-tint-04.$description: Renamed from --accent-system-tint-04 (a one-off accent.base-derived tint distinct from the tint.accent.* scale, which is built from accent.light instead); used by operator.css.

bg.$description: Equals bg.primary (light) — welcome modal has no CRT vignette in light mode, so it just matches the page background.

wt-header-height.$description: Theme-independent layout constant — identical to dark's value.

wt-status-bar-height.$description: Theme-independent layout constant — identical to dark's value.

wt-transition-fast.$description: Theme-independent motion constant — identical to dark's value.

wt-transition-normal.$description: Theme-independent motion constant — identical to dark's value.

wt-accent-system-tint-04.$description: Light theme's accent.base (#0a8f8c = rgb(10,143,140)) at the same 4% alpha as dark's.
